exemplar.title: How did post-9/11 U.S. presidential speeches contribute to the development of discriminative language in American political discourse?

detailedExemplarData.title: How did post-9/11 U.S. presidential speeches contribute to the development of discriminative language in American political discourse?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The research question is clearly formulated and consistently revisited throughout the report, anchoring each section to the investigationâs focus.
Section headings explicitly link each presidential case study back to the research question, maintaining clarity and coherence of purpose.
The introduction and conclusion effectively articulate the studyâs significance, reinforcing the investigationâs focus on discriminatory language.
weaknesses: The methodology description remains at a general level (âanalysing speechesâ) without an explicit analytical framework or coding scheme for identifying âdiscriminative language.â
The opening background material lacks a concise topic sentence directly anchoring it to the research question, leading to minor digressions.
The rationale for selecting Trumpâs 2017 inaugural address is asserted but not academically justified, limiting methodological transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic communication and research question) demonstrate clear, effective, and sustained focus in the 5â6 band, while the methodology strand shows only partial articulation (3â4 band). Holistically, the criterion merited a strong mid-band mark of 5, reflecting a coherent purpose and focused question with a need for more methodological rigor.

strengths: Quotations from the three speeches are clearly relevant and appropriately support the development of the argument.
Key terminology (e.g., âbinary framing,â âIslamophobic rhetoricâ) is used accurately and consistently within the context of political discourse analysis.
weaknesses: The reliance on general web sources (e.g., Wikipedia) undermines the academic depth of the literature base; more peer-reviewed scholarship is needed.
In-text citations and bibliography entries are uneven, and the integration of secondary sources remains largely illustrative rather than critically comparative.
Background, literature review, and purpose are merged in the introduction, limiting the clarity of structured knowledge presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of knowledge and understanding fall securely in the 3â4 band (mostly relevant application, clear but surface-level subject knowledge, accurate terminology). A mark of 4 reflects coherent and consistent use of knowledge and terms, with room to deepen source engagement and structural clarity.

strengths: Close reading of Bushâs âwith us or with the terroristsâ binary framing shows clear analytical engagement and supports cause-and-effect reasoning.
Recognition of Obamaâs tension between unity rhetoric and underlying security framing demonstrates insightful evaluation within the comparative framework.
Analysis of Trumpâs explicit references to âradical Islamic terrorismâ effectively highlights the shift toward overt discriminatory language.
weaknesses: The secondary research base is narrow, lacking peer-reviewed debates or counter-perspectives that would broaden the investigationâs relevance.
At times the analysis overgeneralises the societal impact of presidential rhetoric without empirical support, leading to occasional logical leaps.
The conclusion ends abruptly, missing a cohesive synthesis of evaluative insights and future research reflections.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (research quality, analysis depth, discussion and evaluation) align with the 4â6 band descriptors. The work demonstrates sound, adequate critical thinking with consistent relevance and emerging evaluation. A mid-to-high band score of 6 rewards its solid analytical foundations while acknowledging limitations in breadth and evidential support.

strengths: A comprehensive table of contents and clear section headings ensure an appropriately complete and navigable structure.
Footnotes, bibliography, and formal elements (title page, word count) are present and generally adhere to IB academic conventions.
weaknesses: Bibliography entries lack consistent indentation and citation style, impeding visual clarity and uniformity.
Superscript footnote numbers occasionally disrupt line flow, affecting readability.
The conclusion merges future research with closing remarks, disrupting the logical separation of structural elements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout strands meet the criteria for the upper 3â4 band. Presentation is coherent, complete, and correctly applied, meriting the top mark of 4 in this criterion given only minor formatting and organisational inconsistencies.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research questionââHow did post-9/11 U.S. presidential speeches contribute to the development of discriminative language in American political discourse?ââis **clearly formulated, focused on a defined corpus (three speeches), and tightly linked to the ensuing analysis**.  The student repeatedly returns to this question at section openings and in the conclusion, signalling coherence between question and discussion.

Some shortcomings remain.  The RQâs breadth (âcontribute to the developmentâ) invites complex socio-political variables that the essayâs limited length cannot fully address, leading to occasional over-generalisation.  Nevertheless, the focus on presidential rhetoric provides a reasonably bounded lens, and the question guides selection of evidence and argument.

Overall clarity and connection to discussion place the strand in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects the solid but not exceptional precision of focus.

The analysis moves beyond description by identifying rhetorical strategies (binary logic, exceptionalism, alarmist framing) and linking them to discriminatory outcomes.  **Cause-and-effect reasoning** is present and generally logical.

Yet depth is constrained: quotations are brief, linguistic features (lexis, modality, metaphors) are not systematically dissected, and counter-arguments are largely absent.  Conclusions sometimes over-generalise societal impact without evidence.  The argument, while coherent, remains at an "adequate" rather than âgoodâ level.

This positions the strand within the 4-6 band; a score of 4 reflects solid but limited analytical depth.

The essay draws on the three speeches as primary texts and supplements them with a handful of scholarly and journalistic sources.  The selected quotations are **generally relevant** to the argument (e.g., Bushâs âwith us or with the terrorists,â Trumpâs âradical Islamic terrorismâ).

Nevertheless, the range is narrowâonly one speech per presidencyâand secondary literature is limited, with some dependence on general websites (Wikipedia, ABA article) rather than academic studies.  Integration tends to be illustrative rather than critically comparative, and referencing, though present, is uneven in depth.

This reflects âmostly relevant and appropriateâ application rather than consistently effective use, meriting a 3-4 band placement; a score of 3 is justified.

Formal elements (title page, word count, table of contents, footnotes, bibliography) are present and generally adhere to academic convention.  Fonts, spacing, and heading hierarchy are clear, and hyperlinks are correctly embedded.

Minor issues persist: in-text superscript footnote numbers occasionally break line flow; some URLs are long and not formatted in hanging indent style; and page numbering cannot be verified from the extract.  Still, the overall layout meets IB expectations.

This corresponds to the 3-4 band for layout accuracy; a score of 3 acknowledges sound but not flawless presentation.

The student demonstrates **clear awareness of post-9/11 political rhetoric** and can summarise key themes such as âus vs. them,â American exceptionalism, and Islamophobia.  The commentary shows understanding of how language constructs national identity and otherness.

Yet the treatment remains surface-level.  Complex theoretical perspectives from discourse analysis, sociolinguistics, or post-colonial studies are largely absent, and claims occasionally rely on assertion rather than sustained evidence.  The brevity of the essay (â1 800 words) limits depth and breadth, leading to descriptive rather than fully analytical coverage.

Overall, knowledge is coherent but only moderately developed, fitting the 3-4 band; a midpoint score of 3 is appropriate.

The essay includes a contents table, clear section headings, introduction, body, conclusion, and bibliography, showing **coherent organisation**.  Transitions between presidential case studies are logical and the argument is easy to follow.

Some structural elements are under-developed: the introduction merges background with literature review; sub-sections lack explicit analytical sub-headings; and the very short overall length means depth within each section is limited.  Nevertheless, the structure is appropriate and complete in form.

These features fit the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects competent but not exemplary structure.

The three speeches chosen are **directly pertinent** to the research question, and the student provides contextual secondary references.  Each primary source is cited correctly, and the essay attempts chronological comparison to trace evolution of discourse.

However, the research base is narrow and lacks depth: only six secondary sources are listed, with limited peer-reviewed scholarship and one Wikipedia citation.  Important counter-perspectives (e.g., speeches that complicate the narrative, scholarly debates on securitisation) are missing.  The investigation therefore achieves adequacy but not breadth or rigour.

These characteristics match the 4-6 band descriptor of âadequate with partial relevanceâ; the lower value of 4 is fitting.

The student states an explicit topic â âthe influence of 9/11 on discriminative language in American political discourseâ â and immediately signals its relevance to English-language political rhetoric.  The introduction frames the significance of post-9/11 presidential speeches and clearly anticipates an investigation of three emblematic addresses (Bush 2001, Obama 2009, Trump 2017).

â¢ Purpose and focus are **evident and remain visible** throughout: each main-body section is anchored to one speech and explicitly linked back to the idea of discriminatory language.  
â¢ Minor lapses do appear (e.g., occasional digressions into historical background, limited depth because of the short overall word count), so communication is not exemplary, but readers never doubt what the study is about or why it matters.

Given the generally clear, appropriate, and consistently sustained focusâbut with some uneven depthâthe work best fits the 5-6 band; a mid-band mark of 5 is warranted.

The student offers a clear overall argument tracing a progression from Bush to Trump and evaluates the success and shortcomings of each presidencyâs rhetoric.  The conclusion synthesises findings and notes limitations, demonstrating **some evaluative thinking**.

Nonetheless, the discussion remains one-sided; alternative interpretations, broader socio-political factors, and empirical data on public reception are not considered.  Logical links between presidential words and public policy are asserted rather than demonstrated with evidence.

The work therefore meets the descriptor of âeffective reasoning with inconsistenciesâ at the 4-6 level; a score of 4 is appropriate.

The student outlines a basic methodâclose reading of three major presidential speeches supported by secondary scholarshipâshowing **some informed selection of primary and secondary sources**.  Footnotes and a bibliography evidence consultation of relevant materials.

However, the methodology lacks explicit detail on analytical framework (e.g., criteria for identifying âdiscriminative language,â coding procedures, or linguistic tools employed).  The rationale for limiting the corpus to one speech per president is acknowledged but not justified academically, and there is no discussion of data collection or limitations beyond a brief note in the conclusion.

These features indicate a methodology that is "mostly complete" yet only partially articulated, aligning with the 3-4 band; a cautious score of 3 is appropriate.

Key termsââbinary framing,â âAmerican exceptionalism,â âIslamophobic rhetoric,â âcivilizational conflictââare **used accurately and in context**.  The student employs rhetorical labels such as âus vs. themâ and ânational mobilizationâ consistently across sections, demonstrating command of basic discourse-analysis vocabulary.

Minor inaccuracies (e.g., conflating ânarrationâ with ânarrativeâ) and limited engagement with specialised linguistic theory prevent full sophistication, but terminology remains mostly precise and supports the argument.

Consequently, the work aligns with the upper end of the 3-4 band; a score of 4 recognises generally accurate and consistent use of terms.

annotations.comment: A comprehensive table of contents is provided, indicating a coherent and complete structure that guides the reader through sections and page numbers.
Page numbering appears consistently in the footer, enhancing navigability and meeting formatting expectations.
The essay title is prominently stated at the start of the report, fulfilling the formatting requirement and immediately informing the reader of the research focus.
The research question is clearly formulated and correctly positioned on the title page, providing a focused inquiry that guides the subsequent analysis.

Methodology is described only as âanalysing speechesâ without specifying criteria for identifying âdiscriminative language.â Introduce an explicit analytical framework or coding scheme to strengthen methodological rigor.

The reliance on Wikipedia and general web sources limits academic depth. Incorporate peer-reviewed journal articles or established discourse-analysis studies to enhance the relevance and authority of supporting evidence.
The student correctly cites a relevant ABA source to frame contemporary anti-Muslim rhetoric, showing appropriate use of secondary material to support contextual arguments.
Verify all URLs for accessibility and consider shortening or hyperlinking long addresses to maintain clean line breaks in footnotes.

The final paragraph extends into future research without clear separation from the conclusion, affecting structural coherence. Consider demarcating a distinct âLimitations and Future Researchâ section.
The discussion of future research is insightful but would benefit from reflective linkage to the studentâs own methodology choices and limitations, enhancing evaluative depth.
The inclusion of a clear word count (âWord count: 1758â) satisfies the formatting requirement and demonstrates awareness of the IB limit. Well done on meeting this criterion.
The recognition of Obamaâs tension between unity rhetoric and underlying security framing illustrates insightful cause-and-effect reasoning in the analysis.
Bibliography heading is formatted correctly, but citation entries lack consistent indentation and use mixed URL styles. Apply a uniform referencing format to improve layout consistency.
Reference entries appear as continuous blocks without hanging indents, making it difficult to distinguish individual sources. Adopt hanging indent formatting for clarity.
The bibliography includes a mix of source types (webpages, SpringerLink, Wikipedia) without consistent stylistic treatment. Standardize citation style (e.g., APA, MLA) across all entries.
Footnotes on primary sources are correctly used, but integrating key bibliographic information into in-text citations would reinforce academic rigor and improve readability.
The main-body section headings clearly link each presidential case study back to the research question, maintaining focus and guiding the reader through the argument.

The introduction provides extensive historical background but lacks a concise topic sentence that directly anchors the discussion to the research question. Consider refocusing the opening to explicitly define the studyâs scope.

While the question is restated, the introduction merges background, literature review, and purpose. Consider separating these elements into distinct subsections to improve clarity and demonstrate structured knowledge.
The section effectively reiterates the research question and articulates its significance, reinforcing focus and maintaining coherence with the stated aim.
The primary text analysis provides rich quotations, yet lacks systematic examination of linguistic features (e.g., modality, metaphor). Introduce consistent criteria to deepen analytical precision.

The studentâs close reading of Bushâs binary framing (âwith us or with the terroristsâ) clearly demonstrates analytical engagement with rhetorical strategy and supports the argument effectively.

Obamaâs Cairo speech analysis recognizes inclusive rhetoric but overlooks counter-examples or oppositional perspectives. Incorporating critique from dissenting scholars could strengthen balance and evaluation.

When introducing Trumpâs speech, the rationale for selecting this specific address could be academically justified. Explain why this inaugural address is the most emblematic example of discriminatory rhetoric.
The analysis of Trumpâs âAmerican carnageâ and âradical Islamic terrorismâ effectively highlights a rhetorical shift toward explicit discrimination, demonstrating clear analytical depth.
The conclusion abruptly ends mid-sentence, undermining the presentation of a cohesive synthesis. Complete the concluding thought to ensure the argument is fully articulated.
The student demonstrates intellectual initiative by proposing diverse future research avenues, showing engagement with broader disciplinary and digital contexts.
Ensure that footnote numbering and line breaks do not disrupt the flow of text. Consistent formatting of superscripts will improve readability.
